question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both French Flair and G2 NORD each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both French Flair and G2 NORD each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

question: Game 3: Any Player Quadra Kill?
description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Quadra Kill occurs when a single player kills 4 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Quadra Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Quadra Kill. A Penta Kill (5 kills in rapid succession) also counts as a Quadra Kill having occurred for the purposes of this market.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Team Heretics Academy and Unicorns Of Love Sexy Edition each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Eintracht Spandau and Galions each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain Baron Nashor prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Eintracht Spandau and Galions each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket round 1 match between Eintracht Spandau and Galions in the EMEA Masters Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 23 at 3:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Eintracht Spandau" if Eintracht Spandau win the match against Galions.

This market will resolve to "Galions" if Galions win the match against Eintracht Spandau.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Eintracht Spandau in this EMEA Masters Playoffs BO3 matchup against Galions, driven by Spandau's flawless 5-0 group stage run and commanding 3-0 quarterfinal sweep over Karmine Corp Academy, highlighting superior macro execution and top laner Jankos' carry threat. Galions scraped through with a 2-1 win over Team Heretics but showed vulnerabilities in jungle pathing and late-game scaling against aggressive comps. No confirmed roster disruptions or patches altering meta significantly; full-strength lineups expected. BO3 format amplifies upset potential if Galions steal map one via early cheese, though Spandau's 75% series win rate in playoffs underscores their edge amid high-stakes pressure.

question: Game 3: Any Player Penta Kill?
description:
This market is about whether any player achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. In League of Legends, a Penta Kill occurs when a single player kills all 5 enemy champions in rapid succession.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player on either team achieves a Penta Kill during Game 3. This market will resolve to "No" if no player achieves a Penta Kill.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether a Penta Kill occurred prior to stoppage. If no Penta Kill occurred, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

question: Game 3: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Game 3 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all champion kills recorded during the match by both Eintracht Spandau and Galions. Executions (deaths to non-champion sources such as turrets, minions, or neutral monsters where no enemy champion receives kill credit) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Game 3, or if Game 3 is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 3 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 3 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay Baron Nashor?
description:
This market is about whether both teams slay the Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. Baron Nashor is a powerful neutral objective in League of Legends that first spawns at 20:00 game time and respawns 6 minutes after each death, granting the slaying team the Hand of Baron buff.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Berlin International Gaming and Karmine Corp Blue each slay Baron Nashor at least once during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay Baron Nashor during Game 1.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

question: Game 1: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Berlin International Gaming and Karmine Corp Blue each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 1. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 1. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Game 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Game 1 begins but is not completed and ends via surrender, this market will resolve based on whether both teams had slain at least one elemental dragon prior to stoppage. If the condition was not met, this market will resolve to "No".

If Game 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

question: Game 2: Both Teams Slay a Dragon?
description:
This market is about whether both teams slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. In League of Legends, elemental dragons (Infernal, Mountain, Ocean, Hextech, Chemtech, and Cloud) spawn in the dragon pit starting at 5:00 game time. After being slain, the next dragon spawns 5 minutes later. Once a team claims the Dragon Soul (by slaying four dragons), all subsequent dragon spawns become Elder Dragons.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Berlin International Gaming and Karmine Corp Blue each slay at least one elemental dragon during Game 2. This market will resolve to "No" if either team (or both teams) does not slay any elemental dragon during Game 2. Only elemental dragons count toward this market — Elder Dragon kills do not count.

This market refers to the LoL Upper bracket round 1 match between Eintracht Spandau and Galions in the EMEA Masters Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 23 at 3:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to "Eintracht Spandau" if Eintracht Spandau win the match against Galions.

This market will resolve to "Galions" if Galions win the match against Eintracht Spandau.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Eintracht Spandau in this EMEA Masters Playoffs BO3 matchup against Galions, driven by Spandau's flawless 5-0 group stage run and commanding 3-0 quarterfinal sweep over Karmine Corp Academy, highlighting superior macro execution and top laner Jankos' carry threat. Galions scraped through with a 2-1 win over Team Heretics but showed vulnerabilities in jungle pathing and late-game scaling against aggressive comps. No confirmed roster disruptions or patches altering meta significantly; full-strength lineups expected. BO3 format amplifies upset potential if Galions steal map one via early cheese, though Spandau's 75% series win rate in playoffs underscores their edge amid high-stakes pressure.

question: First Blood in Game 1?
description:
In the upcoming Upper bracket round 1 match between Team Heretics Academy and Unicorns Of Love Sexy Edition in the EMEA Masters Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 23 at 12:00PM ET:

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

question: Will MrBeast's next video get less than 40 million views on week 1?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of views the next YouTube video posted by MrBeast after this market's creation gets in the first 7 days after being posted.

If MrBeast does not post a YouTube video by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this is MrBeast's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's next video posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders peg the 60-70 million view bin at 26% implied probability for MrBeast's next YouTube video in its first week, closely trailed by 70-80M (20.5%) and 80-90M (18.5%), signaling tempered optimism amid his recent track record of 50-75 million week-one hauls on mega-budget challenges like "$1 vs $1,000,000,000 Yacht!" and survival stunts. Key drivers include stabilizing subscriber growth past 300 million, algorithm favoritism for high-engagement spectacle, and cross-promo boosts from Beast Burger and Feastables, though escalating production costs risk viewer fatigue against rivals like MrBeast Gaming spin-offs. Differentiation hinges on the upload's viral hook—teased extreme giveaways or philanthropy twists—versus seasonal streaming competition, with resolution tracking official YouTube analytics through day seven post-premiere.

question: Tucker Carlson federally charged?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the United States federal government formally charges or announces a criminal indictment of Tucker Carlson between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 90% implied probability against federal charges for Tucker Carlson, primarily due to the complete absence of any official Department of Justice announcements, indictments, or public investigations targeting the former Fox News host. Recent developments, including Carlson's independent podcast tour and Putin interview, have drawn partisan criticism but no prosecutorial response from federal agencies. Without new catalysts such as leaked documents or special counsel actions, traders view charges as improbable amid broader First Amendment protections for media figures.
